name: Chocolate Bread Pudding (2)
recipeInstructions:
1. Butter four 1-cup ramekins or six custard cups and place them on a cookie sheet.
2. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
3. Combine the melted chocolate, sugar, salt, and cinnamon in a bowl. Whisk together slowly with milk, then add eggs. Beat the mixture until well blended.
4. Stir in bread cubes and let soak for a few minutes.
5. Beat again; bread cubes should fall apart slightly.
6. Divide pudding among ramekins or custard cups. Place all on cookie sheet.
7. Bake 15 minutes or until edges are spongy (like a cake) and middle is just under cooked.
8. Cool 15 minutes, then serve or cool completely before refrigerating. Refrigerate up to 3-4 days; reheat in microwave at medium power.
The original recipe had half the cinnamon and eggs. Adding an extra egg makes it slightly custardy. Guittard's Vanilla Milk Chips can replace chocolate for a different flavor. Use crustless bread (Pepperidge Farm’s white works well) or whole wheat granola bread for variety. For richer pudding, substitute leftover cake pieces; one layer doubles the batch.
